caption:
The AI gave you a 400-word essay when you asked a yes/no question. Again.

This isn't an AI problem. It's a calibration problem.

Here's what's actually happening: the default AI is trained to be thorough. Comprehensive. Helpful to everyone. Which means it's optimized for no one.

If you're a bullet-point thinker who needs decisions fast, you're getting buried in paragraphs built for someone who wants to understand every angle first.

If you're a big-picture person, you're getting granular step-by-step breakdowns that make your brain glaze over.

The fix isn't reprompting the same question five different ways until you get something usable.

The fix is the AI knowing, before you even ask, that you think in frameworks not footnotes. That you want the 'so what' before the 'here's why.' That three options is decision paralysis but two options is a no-brainer.

That's the difference between a generic AI response and one that's actually calibrated to how YOUR brain works.

Stop fighting the default. Change it.
hook: The AI gave you a 400-word essay when you asked a yes/no question. Again.
